steps.title: Find the x-coordinate
steps.content: Use the linear equation first: $$x+6=16$$ $$x=10$$ So the intersection must have $x=10$.

steps.title: Find the y-coordinate
steps.content: Substitute $x=10$ into the other equation: $$y=3(10)^2+4=3(100)+4=304$$ The solution to the system is the point that satisfies both equations, so the graphs intersect at $(10,304)$. That is Choice C — $(10, 304)$.
